title: festuca gautieri subsp. scoparia
intro: Festuca gautieri subsp. scoparia, also known as Scoparia fescue or Festuca scoparia, is a subspecies of a perennial grass native to the Iberian Peninsula and the Pyrenees. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Festuca gautieri subsp. scoparia include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable resource for herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction for medicinal purposes, with its leaves and stems used in herbal preparations.
